**Accessibility Statement**

The Institute of Hydrobiology at Dresden University of Technology strives to ensure that its website, published at [https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/](https://weblab.hydro.tu-dresden.de/) in accordance with the provisions of the Accessible Websites Act (BfWebG) in conjunction with the Accessible Information Technology Ordinance (BITV 2.0).

**Creation of this Accessibility Statement**

This statement was created on September 9, 2020, and last updated on June 01, 2026. This accessibility statement is based on a self-assessment of our apps conducted on September 28, 2020, by the Chair of Human-Computer Interaction at TU Dresden.

**Current Status of Accessibility**

This website is not yet compliant with the requirements of the BfWebG in conjunction with BITV 2.0. The non-compliances and/or exceptions are listed below.

**Non-Accessible Content**

* Language markup is missing in some parts of the web pages.
* In some cases, page titles are missing or ambiguous.
* The hierarchy of heading levels has not been followed in some instances.
* Screen reader feedback is insufficient in some places:
    - Some form fields lack a link to their label.
    - In some dropdown lists, the entries are not read aloud. 

There are no alternative texts for the graphics generated by the web tools provided. Since these tools are experimental research prototypes, this content is temporarily not designed to be accessible due to a disproportionate burden under Section 2(3) of the BfWebG. For tactile alternatives, please contact the [Disability and Student Services Working Group](https://tu-dresden.de/tu-dresden/chancengleichheit/agsbs) if needed.

**Contact**

If you notice any issues regarding accessibility or if you require information regarding non-accessible content, please contact Dr. Thomas Petzoldt (Email: thomas.petzoldt@tu-dresden.de, Phone: +49 351 463 34954). We appreciate your feedback and support.

We will endeavor to rectify the reported issues or provide you with inaccessible information in an accessible format.

**Enforcement Procedure**

If you feel that we have not addressed your feedback satisfactorily, you may contact the Saxon Enforcement Agency:

Commissioner of the Saxon State Government for the Interests of People with Disabilities, 

Albertstraße 10, 01097 Dresden<br>
Mailing address: Archivstraße 1, 01097 Dresden<br>
Email: info.behindertenbeauftragter@sk.sachsen.de<br> 
Phone: 0351 564-12161<br>
Fax: 0351 564-12169<br>
[Website: https://www.inklusion.sachsen.de](Website: https://www.inklusion.sachsen.de)
